heading: Resource advisory committees
label: 7125 Resource advisory committees

children.heading: Establishment and purpose of resource advisory committees
children.label: a Establishment and purpose of resource advisory committees

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall establish and maintain resource advisory committees to perform the duties in subsection (b), except as provided in paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: to improve collaborative relationships; and

children.label: B
children.text: to provide advice and recommendations to the land management agencies consistent with the purposes of this subchapter.

children.heading: Access to resource advisory committees
children.label: 3 Access to resource advisory committees
children.text: To ensure that each unit of Federal land has access to a resource advisory committee, and that there is sufficient interest in participation on a committee to ensure that membership can be balanced in terms of the points of view represented and the functions to be performed, the Secretary concerned may, establish resource advisory committees for part of, or 1 or more, units of Federal land.1So in original. The comma probably should not appear.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An advisory committee that meets the requirements of this section, a resource advisory committee established before , or an advisory committee determined by the Secretary concerned before , to meet the requirements of this section may be deemed by the Secretary concerned to be a resource advisory committee for the purposes of this subchapter.December 20, 2023December 20, 2023

children.heading: Charter
children.label: B Charter
children.text: A charter for a committee described in subparagraph (A) that was filed on or before , shall be considered to be filed for purposes of this chapter.December 20, 2023

children.heading: Bureau of land management advisory committees
children.label: C Bureau of land management advisory committees
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior may deem a resource advisory committee meeting the requirements of subpart 1784 of part 1780 of title 43, Code of Federal Regulations, as a resource advisory committee for the purposes of this subchapter.
review projects proposed under this subchapter by participating counties and other persons;
propose projects and funding to the Secretary concerned under ;section 7123 of this title
provide early and continuous coordination with appropriate land management agency officials in recommending projects consistent with purposes of this chapter under this subchapter;

provide frequent opportunities for citizens, organizations, tribes, land management agencies, and other interested parties to participate openly and meaningfully, beginning at the early stages of the project development process under this subchapter;

children.label: A
children.text: monitor projects that have been approved under ; andsection 7124 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: advise the designated Federal official on the progress of the monitoring efforts under subparagraph (A); and
make recommendations to the Secretary concerned for any appropriate changes or adjustments to the projects being monitored by the resource advisory committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall appoint the members of resource advisory committees for a term of 4 years beginning on the date of appointment.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: B Reappointment
children.text: The Secretary concerned may reappoint members to subsequent 4-year terms.

children.heading: Basic requirements
children.label: 2 Basic requirements
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall ensure that each resource advisory committee established meets the requirements of subsection (d).

children.heading: Initial appointment
children.label: 3 Initial appointment
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary concerned shall make initial appointments to the resource advisory committees.October 3, 2008

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 4 Vacancies
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall make appointments to fill vacancies on any resource advisory committee as soon as practicable after the vacancy has occurred.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 5 Compensation
children.text: Members of the resource advisory committees shall not receive any compensation.

children.heading: Number
children.label: 1 Number
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (6), each resource advisory committee shall be comprised of 15 members.

children.label: i
children.text: represent organized labor or non-timber forest product harvester groups;

children.label: ii
children.text: represent developed outdoor recreation, off highway vehicle users, or commercial recreation activities;

children.label: I
children.text: energy and mineral development interests; or

children.label: II
children.text: commercial or recreational fishing interests;

children.label: iv
children.text: represent the commercial timber industry; or

children.label: v
children.text: hold Federal grazing or other land use permits, or represent nonindustrial private forest land owners, within the area for which the committee is organized.

children.label: i
children.text: nationally recognized environmental organizations;

children.label: ii
children.text: regionally or locally recognized environmental organizations;

children.label: iii
children.text: dispersed recreational activities;

children.label: iv
children.text: archaeological and historical interests; or

children.label: v
children.text: nationally or regionally recognized wild horse and burro interest groups, wildlife or hunting organizations, or watershed associations.

children.label: i
children.text: hold State elected office (or a designee);

children.label: ii
children.text: hold county or local elected office;

children.label: iii
children.text: represent American Indian tribes within or adjacent to the area for which the committee is organized;

children.label: iv
children.text: are school officials or teachers; or

children.label: v
children.text: represent the affected public at large.

children.heading: Balanced representation
children.label: 3 Balanced representation
children.text: In appointing committee members from the 3 categories in paragraph (2), the Secretary concerned shall provide for balanced and broad representation from within each category.

children.heading: Geographic distribution
children.label: 4 Geographic distribution
children.text: The members of a resource advisory committee shall reside within the State in which the committee has jurisdiction and, to the extent practicable, the Secretary concerned shall ensure local representation in each category in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 5 Chairperson
children.text: A majority on each resource advisory committee shall select the chairperson of the committee.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: A Notice
children.text: On notice from the applicable regional forester that an adequate number of qualified candidates are not interested or available to serve on a resource advisory committee, the Secretary concerned shall publish a notice in the Federal Register seeking candidates for the resource advisory committee.

children.label: i
children.text: the membership requirement under paragraph (1) to not fewer than 9; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the membership requirements under subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (2) to 3 in each category described in that paragraph, except that where a vacancy exists on a resource advisory committee, the Secretary concerned may not reject a qualified applicant from any category.

children.heading: Termination of authority
children.label: C Termination of authority
children.text: The authority provided under this paragraph terminates on .October 1, 2026

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), each resource advisory committee shall establish procedures for proposing projects to the Secretary concerned under this subchapter.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 2 Quorum
children.text: A quorum must be present to constitute an official meeting of the committee.

children.heading: Approval by majority of members
children.label: 3 Approval by majority of members
children.text: A project may be proposed by a resource advisory committee to the Secretary concerned under , if the project has been approved by a majority of members of the committee from each of the 3 categories in subsection (d)(2).section 7123(a) of this title

children.heading: Other committee authorities and requirements
children.label: f Other committee authorities and requirements

children.heading: Staff assistance
children.label: 1 Staff assistance
children.text: A resource advisory committee may submit to the Secretary concerned a request for periodic staff assistance from Federal employees under the jurisdiction of the Secretary.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 2 Meetings
children.text: All meetings of a resource advisory committee shall be announced at least 1 week in advance in a local newspaper of record and shall be open to the public.

children.heading: Records
children.label: 3 Records
children.text: A resource advisory committee shall maintain records of the meetings of the committee and make the records available for public inspection.

children.heading: Resource advisory committee appointment pilot programs
children.label: g Resource advisory committee appointment pilot programs

children.heading: Applicable designee
children.label: A Applicable designee
children.text: The term “applicable designee” means the applicable regional forester.

children.heading: National pilot program
children.label: B National pilot program
children.text: The term “national pilot program” means the national pilot program established under paragraph (4)(A).

children.heading: Regional pilot program
children.label: C Regional pilot program
children.text: The term “regional pilot program” means the regional pilot program established under paragraph (3)(A).

children.heading: Establishment of pilot programs
children.label: 2 Establishment of pilot programs
children.text: In accordance with paragraphs (3) and (4), the Secretary concerned shall carry out 2 pilot programs to appoint members of resource advisory committees.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall carry out a regional pilot program to allow an applicable designee to appoint members of resource advisory committees.

children.label: i
children.text: the State of Montana; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the State of Arizona.

children.heading: Review
children.label: i Review
children.text: Before appointing a member of a resource advisory committee under the regional pilot program, an applicable designee shall conduct the review and analysis that would otherwise be conducted for an appointment to a resource advisory committee if the regional pilot program was not in effect, including any review and analysis with respect to civil rights and budgetary requirements.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: ii Savings clause
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph relieves an applicable designee from any requirement developed by the Secretary concerned for making an appointment to a resource advisory committee that is in effect on , including any requirement for advertising a vacancy.December 20, 2018

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall carry out a national pilot program to allow the Chief of the Forest Service or the Director of the Bureau of Land Management, as applicable, to submit to the Secretary concerned nominations of individuals for appointment as members of resource advisory committees.

children.label: i
children.text: appoint the nominee to the applicable resource advisory committee; or

children.label: ii
children.text: reject the nomination.

children.heading: Automatic appointment
children.label: C Automatic appointment
children.text: If the Secretary concerned does not act on a nomination in accordance with subparagraph (B) by the date described in that subparagraph, the nominee shall be deemed appointed to the applicable resource advisory committee.

children.label: i
children.text: the State of Montana; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the State of Arizona.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: E Savings clause
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph relieves the Secretary concerned from any requirement relating to an appointment to a resource advisory committee, including any requirement with respect to civil rights or advertising a vacancy.

children.heading: Termination of effectiveness
children.label: 5 Termination of effectiveness
children.text: The authority provided under this subsection terminates on .October 1, 2026
